retry_reason: Passing NULL is a safe no-op; putting a valid object always succeeds (refcount decremented). Retrying would introduce a double-put and is dangerous.
contract_summary: Releases one reference on a struct btf. If the reference count drops to zero, the BTF ID is freed and the object is scheduled for RCU-free (btf_free_rcu). Calling btf_put on a NULL pointer is safely ignored. After calling btf_put, the caller must not access the btf object unless they hold another valid reference. There is no return value to check; the caller must rely on their own reference tracking.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to decrement refcount and free the BTF object when refcount reaches zero; minimal additional logic (NULL check and cleanup calls on zero path).

check.reason: The refcount_dec_and_test call operates on &btf->refcnt, which corresponds to the access path btf.refcnt.refs starting from parameter btf. The type chain btf--refcount_t--atomic_t correctly traces the struct btf, its field refcnt (refcount_t), and the internal refs field (atomic_t). Segment counts match (3 each), and the operation direction 'put' is consistent with the callee information.
